requirements.title: Private stash scan publishes a complete v2-backed snapshot without losing the last good publish on failure

behaviors.title: scan start returns scan metadata for the selected stash scope
scan status reports running progress and the current published scan
private scan writes v2 latest-item and history rows for every scanned item
private scan publishes only after v2 rows are written and before legacy compatibility writes
valuation failure during a scan preserves the last good published status
v2 write failure marks the scan failed and does not publish partial data
ordinary stash scan status ignores valuation-refresh lifecycle rows

requirements.title: Backend price quality classification is canonical and user-visible

behaviors.title: valuation math normalizes non-chaos listed prices before computing price bands
absolute delta thresholds map to good, mediocre, and bad price bands
published stash items expose backend price band, evaluation label, and band version
latest valuation result payload keeps every scanned item and its price quality fields

requirements.title: Published stash snapshot and item history read paths prefer the optimized item-centric model

behaviors.title: published stash reads resolve the latest published scan before reading latest-item rows
published stash results include every published tab and every published item for the resolved scan
published stash results preserve stored chaos values for non-computable currencies
published stash results fall back to legacy valuation rows only when v2 latest rows are absent
item history route returns lineage header metadata plus history entries
item history reads v2 rows with the frozen 90 day retention window
item history prefers v2 rows after backfill
item history falls back to legacy valuation rows only when v2 history is absent
published stash scanStatus ignores valuation-refresh lifecycle rows

requirements.title: Valuation refresh uses persisted published rows and reconciles cache entries against published scan identity

behaviors.title: valuation refresh starts from persisted rows for the current published scan without requiring live stash access
valuation refresh start deduplicates against the persisted active refresh after cache loss
valuation refresh start evicts stale pending cache entries when the published scan changes
valuation refresh status ignores ordinary active scans
valuation refresh status ignores refresh rows tied to an older source scan
valuation refresh status prefers persisted running progress over same-scan pending cache
valuation refresh republishes the latest scan from persisted v2 rows
valuation refresh status recovers the latest published refresh after cache clear
valuation refresh result reconstructs the latest published payload after cache clear
valuation refresh status evicts stale completed cache entries when the published scan changes
valuation refresh result evicts stale completed cache entries when the published scan changes
failed valuation refresh status preserves the last good result payload
persisted valuation refresh fails closed when recovered source rows are partial
valuation refresh start rechecks the current published scan after lock before reusing pending cache
valuation refresh status rechecks the current published scan after lock before reusing cached status
valuation refresh result rechecks the current published scan after lock before reusing cached results
valuation refresh can reuse a published valuation-refresh snapshot as the persisted source for the next refresh

requirements.title: Schema migrations preserve the v2 query path, retention, backfill, and refresh lifecycle contract

behaviors.title: migration 0089 defines v2 stash tables, latest-item view, and query-path TTLs
migration 0090 applies 90 day retention to private stash task data
migration 0091 backfill normalizes chaos values for v2 reads
migration 0092 adds backend price evaluation columns
migration 0093 adds valuation refresh lifecycle kind and source metadata

requirements.title: API contract and client normalization preserve the stash-scan price-quality surface

behaviors.title: TypeScript API normalization preserves backend price judgments from stash scan results
OpenAPI includes the canonical stash lifecycle routes
OpenAPI describes published stash result price-quality fields
OpenAPI describes valuation refresh lifecycle metadata
OpenAPI describes item history price-quality fields

requirements.title: The stash viewer visibly preserves backend good-or-not-good judgments

behaviors.title: stash viewer keeps backend price judgments after valuation results merge into the current stash view
stash viewer falls back to backend price band when the evaluation label is omitted
item history dialog shows backend price quality
